input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Install Xcode (iOS requirement)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- After install, run: `xcode-select --install` (command-line tools)
- Accept the license: `sudo xcodebuild -license accept`
- Open Xcode once to finish component installation
- **Version discovery:** MAUI docs specify minimum Xcode version per .NET release — check [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation)

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest LTS or STS release
- Or use Homebrew: `brew install --cask dotnet-sdk`
- **Verify:** `dotnet --version`

## 3. Install the MAUI workload

```bash
dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, and platform dependencies
- **Discover available versions:** `dotnet workload search maui`
- **Verify:** `dotnet workload list` — should show `maui` as installed

## 4. Install Android SDK components

The MAUI workload bundles the Android SDK manager, but you need to ensure the right pieces are present:

```bash
dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies -f net9.0-android \
  -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$HOME/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx" \
  -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
```

Alternatively, install **Android Studio** ([developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)) for SDK Manager UI and emulator images.

**Key SDK components needed:**
- Android SDK Platform (API level matching your `TargetFramework`, e.g., API 35)
- Android SDK Build-Tools
- Android Emulator + a system image (for testing)

Set `ANDROID_HOME` if using a custom path:
```bash
export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
```

## 5. (Optional but recommended) Install an IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

> *Visual Studio for Mac has been retired — VS Code or Rider are the current paths on macOS.*

## 6. Verify the full toolchain

```bash
# Check .NET and workloads
dotnet --info
dotnet workload list

# Create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for both targets
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-ios
```

## 7. Version compatibility cheat-sheet

| Check | Command / Location |
|-------|-------------------|
| Required Xcode version | [MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) |
| Supported Android API levels | `dotnet new maui` template's `.csproj` `TargetFramework` |
| .NET SDK vs workload compat | `dotnet workload update` keeps them in sync |

---

**TL;DR install order:** Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → Android SDK (via workload or Android Studio) → verify with `dotnet new maui` + build. Always check the [official MAUI installation docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation) for the exact version matrix matching your .NET release.
